comment.body:
I want to invite you to an APRIL CHALLENEGE
Let's be more conscious with the money.
It is not financial advise, it is just a game ;))

Wealthy plp invest the majority of their savings rather than spending on luxury goods that serve as status symbols. 
They focus on assets that appreciate over time—stocks, real estate, businesses, or diversified index funds.
Tax-deferred retirement accounts, strategic asset allocation, and sometimes even trusts, keeping a low profile in spending minimizes lifestyle inflation that can attract unnecessary tax or social pressures.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vOhMhri31WM :))))

The key is: 
Income = savings + Necessary expenses + discretionary spending
Determine your target savings rate and stick to it. If your expenses exceed your targets, adjust either by cutting non-essential costs or recalculating your percentages.

1) Monitor your YoY monthly spendings, and calculate the income/spending ratio % by cathegories: necessities (actual necessities, like housing, food, transportation, and the necessary amount only! don't cheat yourself), discretionary spendings, savings, and investments (including trading), and check the "left-tail" spendings in avg and its SD to check the sudden and necessary spendings (e.g. fire, hospital, broken car, etc.)
And adjust inflation

2) Lets try “safe withdrawal rate” of your accumulated net worth. The idea is to have enough assets so that even if you stop earning an income, your lifestyle can be maintained by withdrawing only a safe percentage each year.
Lets say your monthly expenses is 30% of your monthly income, you can calculate the annual expenses. 
Then divide it by 0.04 (as the commmon safe withdrawal rate is 4%), and this will give you the required net worth, in case you  wish to eventually fund your lifestyle solely from your investments. 

3) Using these datas, lets use budgeting rules (like a modified 50/30/20 or even more aggressive splits) that break down expenses into fixed needs, discretionary spending, and savings + investments. 
For example, with 70% saved: 
- necessities 25% of your monthly income
- discretionary spendings (5% of your income)
The rest is saving. And based on the historical data, you separate some amount for emergency (based on the historical SD upper lvls). 

4) For trading, always have a monthly amount of money that you put into work in the market. And calculate the daily amounts. 
Have a min profit target and a max loss target, and before trading, always have a plan: decide what strategy (long, short, directional or premium collecting, etc.) you will do based on the market state, what is your target entry, what signs you are looking for to prove or disprove you, and what is the betsize you are willing to give for that bet. And then lets be sticked to it.
Never deviate.
With this, you can make a safe and steady growth.
Read my post about risk managemnet.

Lets do this challenge into April as you wish, 
make financial plans for the next month, its weeks, and its days,
And lets recheck next EOM, how it was going. :))

comment.body: the other thing to think about is who benefits in a high tariff world. I think big internationals will not do as well as smaller companies who make most of their revenue from USA. also contsrtuction and infrastructure providers, like lights, electrics , windows  has to be a big winner. Big companies are going to fact retaliatory sanctions and slowing global trade.... small companies are going to be building out infrastructure etc for all these new factories. I think IWM could soar...its at low multiples anyway

comment.body:
Based on OD 
SPX RangeLogic Overlay – Friday, Mar 28
CHOP ZONE: 5675–5715
• Hold 5675–5715 → chop likely
• >5700 <5715 = resistance from short put unwind & 5715 minor lid
• <5700 >5675 = mean reversion expected
• Drift = pin near 5700
ABOVE CHOP
• >5715 → opens 5730
• >5730 → breakout to 5740 (put/call peak)
• >5740 → run to 5765
• >5765 → test 5770 gamma flip
⚠️ Above 5770 = slower grind, positive gamma choke
BELOW CHOP
• <5675 → test 5650
• <5650 → gap to 5640, then 5630
⚠️ 5640 = gamma drop → speed risk
⚠️ 5630 = behavioral shift → stall risk
• <5630 → 5610 → 5600 gamma flip
• <5600 → 5560 = strong support
RECLAIM LOGIC
• 5650 > 5675 → bounce to 5700
• 5630 > 5650 → range resumes
• 5610 > 5630 → bounce to 5650/75
• Fail above 5730 <5740 → fade to 5700 pin
EXTREMES
• >5770 = gamma flip → grind risk
• <5600 = dealer flip → flush to 5560
#SPX #0DTE #GammaOverlay #SPXFlow #OptionsFlow #Vanna #Charm #SPXRoadmap #MarketMap #FridayFlow

comment.body:
You make an excellent point! I think Warren Buffett, despite being a financial genius, seems to have fallen into the classic trap of wealth accumulation that can sometimes miss the deeper psychological aspects of money's utility.
I see money mainly as a tool be being free. However, once you reach to this stage, we have to take into account the non-linear utility of money. Essentially, the marginal value of each additional dollar decreases as your wealth increases. The difference between $0 and $10,000 is life-changing. The difference between $10 million and $11 million? Not so much.
